title: Phoenix Wright: Ace Attorney
creator: Shu Takumi
notes: Original GBA/DS game featuring five cases. The DS version adds the bonus case Rise from the Ashes.

persons.name: Phoenix Wright
persons.notes: Defense attorney, main playable character.

persons.name: Mia Fey
persons.notes: Phoenix's mentor and senior attorney at Fey & Co. Law Offices.

persons.name: Miles Edgeworth
persons.notes: Ruthless prosecutor, later revealed to have a complex past.

persons.name: Manfred von Karma
persons.notes: Legendary prosecutor and true villain of the game.

persons.name: Cindy Stone
persons.notes: Frank Sahwit's victim in The First Turnabout.

persons.name: Frank Sahwit
persons.notes: Killer in The First Turnabout; posed as a newspaper salesman.

persons.name: Maya Fey
persons.notes: Mia's younger sister, spirit medium, and Phoenix's assistant.

persons.name: Redd White
persons.notes: CEO of Bluecorp; killed Mia Fey to silence her.

persons.name: Jack Hammer
persons.notes: Veteran actor on the set of Steel Samurai.

persons.name: Dee Vasquez
persons.notes: Studio producer; killed Jack Hammer in self-defense.

persons.name: Robert Hammond
persons.notes: Defense attorney shot dead at Gourd Lake.

persons.name: Yogi (Yanni Yogi)
persons.notes: Former bailiff who killed Robert Hammond out of revenge.

persons.name: Gregory Edgeworth
persons.notes: Miles Edgeworth's father; killed in the DL-6 Incident elevator.

persons.name: Neil Marshall
persons.notes: Detective killed by Joe Darke; his death was covered up by Damon Gant. (Rise from the Ashes)

persons.name: Joe Darke
persons.notes: Serial killer executed for multiple murders; accidentally killed Neil Marshall.

persons.name: Damon Gant
persons.notes: Chief of police who killed Neil Marshall to conceal evidence and blackmail Lana. (Rise from the Ashes)

persons.name: Lana Skye
persons.notes: Chief prosecutor; Ema's sister, blackmailed by Damon Gant. (Rise from the Ashes)

persons.name: Bruce Goodman
persons.notes: Detective murdered by Damon Gant when he tried to re-open the SL-9 case. (Rise from the Ashes)

deaths.notes: Case 1 – The First Turnabout. Cindy was struck with a clock while Frank Sahwit was robbing her apartment.
Case 2 – Turnabout Sisters. Mia was struck with a glass trophy by Redd White to prevent her from exposing him.
Case 3 – Turnabout Samurai. Jack Hammer fell onto a fence spike after Dee Vasquez pushed him in self-defense during his attempt on her life.
Case 4 – Turnabout Goodbyes. Yogi shot Hammond at Gourd Lake as revenge for Hammond's poor defense that left him faking insanity for 15 years.
Case 4 – DL-6 Incident (15 years prior). Manfred von Karma shot Gregory Edgeworth in a stalled elevator, framing Yanni Yogi.
Case 5 – Rise from the Ashes (SL-9 Incident). Joe Darke accidentally killed detective Neil Marshall during a struggle.
Case 5 – Rise from the Ashes. Damon Gant stabbed Neil Marshall a second time to ensure his death and fabricate evidence to blackmail Lana Skye.
Case 5 – Rise from the Ashes. Damon Gant killed Detective Goodman to prevent the SL-9 case from being re-investigated.

title: Phoenix Wright: Ace Attorney – Justice for All
creator: Shu Takumi
notes: Second game in the series, featuring four cases including the fan-favourite Farewell, My Turnabout.

persons.name: Phoenix Wright
persons.notes: Defense attorney, main playable character.

persons.name: Mia Fey
persons.notes: Phoenix's deceased mentor; channels through Maya during some cases.

persons.name: Maya Fey
persons.notes: Phoenix's assistant and spirit medium; held hostage in Case 4.

persons.name: Miles Edgeworth
persons.notes: Prosecutor who returns in Case 4 to assist Phoenix.

persons.name: Turner Grey
persons.notes: Doctor killed during a spirit channeling session in Case 1.

persons.name: Mimi Miney
persons.notes: Nurse who killed Turner Grey; posed as her dead sister Morgan's spirit.

persons.name: Morgan Fey
persons.notes: Fey clan elder who orchestrated the spirit channeling trap; Pearl's mother.

persons.name: Russell Berry
persons.notes: Ringmaster of the Gatewater Land circus, found dead in Case 2.

persons.name: Acro (Ken Dingling)
persons.notes: Circus acrobat who killed Russell Berry by mistake while aiming at Regina.

persons.name: Regina Berry
persons.notes: Russell Berry's daughter and animal tamer; Acro's intended target.

persons.name: Bat (Billy Bite)
persons.notes: Acro's younger brother who was mauled by a lion while trying to prank Regina; in a coma.

persons.name: Juan Corrida
persons.notes: Rival actor to Matt Engarde; killed in Case 4 by Shelly de Killer.

persons.name: Matt Engarde
persons.notes: Actor who hired the assassin Shelly de Killer to murder Juan Corrida.

persons.name: Shelly de Killer
persons.notes: Professional assassin hired by Matt Engarde to kill Juan Corrida.

persons.name: Adrian Andrews
persons.notes: Manager of Juan Corrida; initially suspected of his murder.

persons.name: Franziska von Karma
persons.notes: Prosecutor in Cases 1–3; Manfred von Karma's daughter.

deaths.notes: Case 1 – Turnabout Reunion. Mimi Miney shot Dr. Grey during a spirit channeling to stop him exposing her role in a medical malpractice incident.
Case 2 – Turnabout Big Top (backstory). Bat was mauled by Leon the lion after Regina playfully gave the lion a pepper-laced treat during Bat's prank; Bat fell into a coma.
Case 2 – Turnabout Big Top. Acro dropped a heavy bust from his window intending to kill Regina Berry but struck her father Russell by mistake.
Case 4 – Farewell, My Turnabout. Juan Corrida was strangled in his hotel room by assassin Shelly de Killer, hired by Matt Engarde.
Case 4 – Farewell, My Turnabout. Maya was held hostage by Shelly de Killer and threatened with execution if Phoenix did not secure a Not Guilty verdict for Engarde; ultimately freed.

title: Phoenix Wright: Ace Attorney – Trials and Tribulations
creator: Shu Takumi
notes: Third and final game in the original trilogy; ties together story threads involving Dahlia Hawthorne across all five cases.

persons.name: Phoenix Wright
persons.notes: Defense attorney, main playable character.

persons.name: Mia Fey
persons.notes: Playable defense attorney in Cases 1 and 4 (set in the past).

persons.name: Miles Edgeworth
persons.notes: Prosecutor in Case 5 who assists Phoenix.

persons.name: Maya Fey
persons.notes: Phoenix's assistant; channels Dahlia's spirit in Case 5.

persons.name: Pearl Fey
persons.notes: Young spirit medium who channeled Dahlia's spirit in Case 5.

persons.name: Dahlia Hawthorne
persons.notes: Primary villain of the game; poisoner and manipulator responsible for multiple deaths.

persons.name: Doug Swallow
persons.notes: Phoenix's college classmate; killed by Dahlia Hawthorne in Case 1.

persons.name: Kane Bullard
persons.notes: CEO killed by his bodyguard Luke Atmey in Case 2.

persons.name: Ron DeLite
persons.notes: Accused of being the thief Mask*DeMasque and suspected of murdering Kane Bullard; actually innocent of murder.

persons.name: Luke Atmey
persons.notes: Self-styled ace detective who murdered Kane Bullard and framed Ron DeLite to cover his own identity as Mask*DeMasque.

persons.name: Glen Elg
persons.notes: Programmer killed by Furio Tigre in Case 3.

persons.name: Furio Tigre
persons.notes: Loan shark who killed Glen Elg and impersonated Phoenix Wright at trial.

persons.name: Valerie Hawthorne
persons.notes: Detective and Dahlia's half-sister; killed by Dahlia in Case 4.

persons.name: Terry Fawles
persons.notes: Escaped convict who was manipulated and killed by Dahlia's poison in Case 4.

persons.name: Misty Fey
persons.notes: Renowned spirit medium and mother of Mia and Maya; killed by Dahlia Hawthorne (channeled through Pearl) in Case 5.

persons.name: Elise Deauxnim
persons.notes: Alias used by Misty Fey; her true identity is revealed posthumously.

persons.name: Godot (Diego Armando)
persons.notes: Prosecutor in Cases 3 and 5; actually killed Misty Fey to stop Dahlia from harming Maya.

persons.name: Morgan Fey
persons.notes: Orchestrated the plan to have Dahlia kill Maya via a channeling through Pearl.

deaths.notes: Case 1 – Turnabout Memories (set in the past). Dahlia pushed Doug into an electrical pole to silence him before he could warn Phoenix about the poison necklace scheme.
Case 2 – The Stolen Turnabout. Luke Atmey killed his blackmailer Kane Bullard and framed Ron DeLite to avoid exposure as Mask*DeMasque.

Case 3 – Recipe for Turnabout. Furio Tigre poisoned Glen Elg with his own experimental antidote compound after Glen won his debt back at poker, then impersonated Phoenix Wright to secure an acquittal.
Case 4 – Turnabout Beginnings (set in the past). Dahlia stabbed her half-sister Valerie, a detective who helped her frame Terry Fawles five years earlier, to prevent further exposure.
Case 4 – Turnabout Beginnings. Terry Fawles swallowed a capsule of poison Dahlia had given him years before as a 'promise' token; he died on the witness stand after realizing Dahlia had betrayed him.

Case 5 – Bridge to the Turnabout. Godot fatally stabbed Misty Fey (who was channeling Dahlia) to prevent Dahlia from killing Maya. He used the alias Elise Deauxnim for Misty Fey. The motive was to protect Maya in his own misguided way.
